title: ValidationError
description: Canonical, transport-friendly error record produced by every validator in the project (CI repo-contract checker, runtime Zod adapter, engine pre-dispatch validator, AI generation feedback loop). Replaces ad-hoc string errors so that editors, IDE integrations, and the Phase-3 AI feedback loop can consume failures generically. Errors are presentation-side only — they do not enter saves, replays, or the canonical state hash.

$schema.description: Must match the `$id` form of the validation-error schema.

packId.description: Pack identifier the offending record belongs to. Optional for repo-wide errors (e.g. CI checks that scan source trees, not pack contents).

recordId.description: Stable record id within the pack (e.g. `unit:emberwild.ash-hound`). Optional when the failure is at the pack level (e.g. manifest validation) or repo level.

schemaId.description: The `$id` of the schema that produced the error (e.g. `heroes-reforged/unit.schema.json`). Lets editors resolve the offending field's documentation.

jsonPointer.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer into the offending record. Use `""` for whole-document errors. Always present so consumers can render a jump-to-source affordance.

rule.description: Closed enum of validator rule kinds. `custom` covers project-specific checks (e.g. cross-pack reference resolution, formula AST whitelist) that do not map to a JSON Schema keyword.

expected.description: Schema-side expectation. May be any JSON-safe value (string, integer, array of allowed enum values, or object describing the expected shape). Optional — some rules (e.g. `additional`) carry the offending value in `actual` only.

actual.description: The value that failed validation. Any JSON-safe value. Optional when the failure is structural (e.g. missing required field has nothing to report).

message.description: Human-readable summary. Stable enough to log; not localized — the localized rendering uses `messageKey` in the consuming UI.

messageKey.description: Optional localization id paired with `message`. When present, UI surfaces should resolve this against the localization table; when absent, fall back to `message`.

severity.description: Mirrors `error-state.schema.json` severity tiers. Defaults to `error` when omitted.

sourcePath.description: Repo-relative path of the file the error came from (e.g. `content-schema/examples/packs/emberwild-faction/units/ash-hound.unit.json`). Optional — set by file-walking validators, omitted by in-memory validators.
